【Daiwa Cautious on Alibaba Profitability but Bullish on AI Monetization】Daiwa analysts said Alibaba’s long-term outlook remains promising despite weaker-than-expected Q3 profitability. They are cautious on overall profitability but constructive on Alibaba Cloud’s medium-term AI monetization, highlighting the new Token Hub unit as a core engine for scalable token-based consumption. Food delivery losses stayed high in the December quarter but are expected to narrow next quarter. Daiwa maintains a buy rating but lowers the target price to HK$184 from HK$191.

【EM Stocks Fluctuate Before Weekend Even as Lower Oil Helps Mood】Emerging-market equities swung Friday after US and Israeli efforts eased Iran war fears, sending oil down from near-four-year highs. MSCI EM index fluctuated following Thursday’s 2.7% drop, while EM currencies rose 0.2%, both set for weekly gains. Barclays’ Mitul Kotecha noted market calm is fragile amid volatile news. Oil eased after Trump ruled out ground troops and Netanyahu pledged no further strikes on Iranian energy facilities, following missile damage to Qatar’s Ras Laffan LNG hub. Reduced Fed easing expectations also weighed on sentiment.

【Musk Team’s Solar Equipment Order Seen Shipping in Early May, Source Says】A Musk-led team is expected to ship a previously ordered batch of solar equipment in the first week of May, a source told Cailian Press. The SpaceX team had placed an order with a leading Chinese heterojunction equipment maker, with exports subject to regulatory filing and approval. Orders are split between SpaceX (S-chain) for space applications and Tesla (T-chain) for ground use. Discussions on T-chain orders with several TOPCon equipment makers are still ongoing, the source said.
IRAN SAID TO ALLOW MORE INDIAN VESSELS PASS STRAIT OF HORMUZ: CNN NEWS 18

【OpenAI Plans Desktop App Combining Chat, Coding and Web Browsing】OpenAI is creating a desktop app that integrates ChatGPT, Codex, and ChatGPT Atlas, its AI-powered web browser, to boost user engagement amid competition from Anthropic PBC. CEO Fidji Simo will lead the integration and marketing, with President Greg Brockman assisting. Launch timing is unclear. The development, first reported by The Wall Street Journal, comes as major AI firms, including Alphabet Inc., release models tackling tasks from code generation to financial analysis and realistic media creation.

【Delton Shares Jump 106% in HK Debut After $421 Million Listing】Delton Technology Guangzhou’s shares surged 106% to HK$147.9 in their Hong Kong debut, after raising HK$3.3B ($421M), before easing. Shenzhen-listed shares fell 3.9%. Investor interest was fueled by China’s AI-related stocks, including Alibaba and Baidu, amid soaring demand for computing and storage. Delton, a printed circuit board maker for servers, is on track for the largest first-day gain in Hong Kong for listings raising over $400M since Montage Technology’s 64% jump in February.

【Tesla Solar Procurement Plan Confirmed by Company】Tesla is planning large-scale purchases of Chinese solar equipment involving several listed companies, according to market chatter. A solar firm confirmed the plan, indicating the contract size could reach gigawatt scale, the Beijing News reported.

【CATL Hong Kong Rally Drives Record Premium Over Mainland Shares】Shares of Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. briefly hit a record Hong Kong premium over China's mainland listings after strong earnings and energy supply shocks. HK shares rose ~35% versus 16% for Shenzhen shares, pushing the gap to 46%, exceeding levels last July. Morgan Stanley notes a 10% premium is typical versus peers like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. and BYD Co., implying current highs may be unsustainable.

【Australia Weighs LNG Windfall Tax as World Faces Soaring Prices】Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has asked Treasury to model a windfall tax on Australia’s LNG sector as global prices surge. Energy Minister Chris Bowen confirmed the request but offered no details, citing cabinet confidentiality. The spike follows Middle East supply disruptions, including missile damage to Qatar’s Ras Laffan hub and Strait of Hormuz blockages. Australia, the world’s third-largest LNG exporter, shipped nearly 80 million tons worth A$65B in 2024–25, with the sector paying about A$22B in taxes and royalties, per Australian Energy Producers.

【Copper and Aluminum Rise as US, Israel Try to Reassure on War】Copper and aluminum rose after Thursday’s steep losses, following US and Israeli moves easing Middle East conflict fears. Netanyahu pledged Israeli forces would avoid energy targets, and Trump ruled out ground troops in Iran. Metals markets have been shaken by potential Gulf supply disruptions, particularly in aluminum, while demand concerns dominate. Rising oil and gas prices threaten global manufacturing, fuel inflation, and could prompt tighter central bank policies. Traders are now watching crude prices, which eased Friday, amid pre-war questions over metals prices versus demand.

【Intel Reportedly Readies a 10% Price Hike for Consumer CPUs】Intel has reportedly informed its major PC clients of a planned 10% price increase on its consumer CPUs. According to industry sources cited by ET News, this price hike will affect Intel's Core Ultra family of processors, which power hundreds of millions of PCs worldwide.

【Pop Mart, Laopu Gold See Bright 2025 Amid Slower China Consumer Spending】Pop Mart International and Laopu Gold are set for triple-digit growth, standing out amid China’s weak consumer spending. Pop Mart shifts to new toy lines like Twinkle Twinkle and Skullpanda, while Laopu benefits from strong gold-linked jewelry demand, per Bloomberg Intelligence. This contrasts with broader retail struggles: Haidilao and Anta likely saw weaker earnings, Meituan faces fierce food delivery competition, and Xiaomi’s smartphone margins are squeezed by high memory costs.
LAOPU GOLD SHARES FALL 5.5% IN HONG KONG

【China Issues Guidelines to Strengthen Protections for Gig Workers】China issued trial guidelines targeting platform companies and their labor partners to regulate labor rules and algorithm use. The measures focus on key areas including order allocation, pay and commissions, working hours and rest, delivery time estimates, routing, and performance evaluation and penalties to better protect gig workers.

【Alibaba, Tencent Shed $66 Billion After AI Vision Falls Flat】Alibaba Group and Tencent lost $66B in market value within 24 hours as investors punished China’s tech giants for unclear AI monetization plans. Alibaba’s US shares fell most since October, Tencent its worst in a year. Heavy AI investments in data centers, talent, and models worry investors amid a consumer downturn and Alibaba’s 67% quarterly net income drop. Analyst Catherine Lim notes markets await measurable revenue from AI via cloud, advertising, or transactions; without clarity, investor caution persists. US hyperscalers spend far more on AI.

>SMCI co-founder Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw arrested today

content.content: some good stuff in the indictment. the anonymous "Company-1" (that I think miiiight be Compuware; a sibling of Liaw owns >8% of Compuware https://www.investing.com/analysis/super-micro-computers-10q-is-loaded-with-red-flags-200648934) was SMCI's 11th most profitable customer in FY24 Q4. there's some great text message details (texting "Heheheh, Gooood news for everyone!" and "Passed!!!" about tricking the compliance team, texting "We need to speed these up before May 13!" because of new Trump export rules taking effect on that day). apparently it required 100 people for the dummy server staging operation, some SMCI compliance auditors were off-site "enjoying entertainment paid for by Company-1" when they received photos/videos of the dummy servers (para 24(d)), a defendant posed as "an assistant to Company-1's local law firm" (para 28).
Smci is fucking hilarious, also I'm flat on the day after being down over 5% at open, let's go!
Tomorrow will be interesting for sure. Busy as hell work wise so I might not be active unfortunately

the indictment also seeks forfeiture of all property derived from the scheme "and substitute assets" if those can't be located. Liaw owns $464 million so that could be an issue. I'd say the indictment looks a lot worse for SMCI's compliance team than the press release. There are more details about the compliance team being tricked pretty badly (Company-1's orders surged, which was flagged by compliance but then the defendant got a "friendly" auditor to conduct the review and Liaw emailed compliance directly telling them to "speed this process up"). There's also fraudulent data center lease agreements that should have been flagged. the compliance team was also fooled by an email by the defendants convincing them that a compliance hold should be lifted. and a defendant said in an email that there was a "high probability" that an audit would only look at B200 GPUs, so it seems he knew ahead of time what the compliance team was going to do (fishy, at best). doesn't look good
in any case, should make for a fun day tomorrow
